pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 8-10 explicitly define WORKSPACE_DIR and PROJECT_DIR variables
setup_task.sh line 11 defines PACKAGE_DIR, line 88 creates Product.java in this location
task.json description lines state 'Ensure two Products are considered equal IF AND ONLY IF they have the same sku'; setup_task.sh line 100 defines sku field with comment 'BUSINESS KEY'
task.json description instructs to 'Uncheck id, name, price, and stockQuantity'; setup_task.sh lines 99,101-103 define these exact mutable/surrogate fields in Product class
task.json description explicitly states 'code must use java.util.Objects.hash and java.util.Objects.equals'; setup_task.sh line 92 imports Objects; verifier.py line 106 checks for Objects.hash usage

privileged_info_summary: The task is to generate hashCode() and equals() methods for a Product class in Eclipse using business key equality. The Product class is located at src/main/java/com/store/model/Product.java in the InventorySystem project at /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/InventorySystem. The required field for equality is 'sku' (the business key), while the forbidden fields are: id, name, price, and stockQuantity. The implementation must use the java.util.Objects.hash and java.util.Objects.equals style methods. The data consists of a synthetically generated Java class with five fields: Long id (surrogate key), String sku (business key), String name, BigDecimal price, and int stockQuantity (all mutable).
